scenarios.title: Python Repository Maintenance
scenarios.when_to_read: Tasks that edit, review, test, document, package, or maintain Python source repositories rather than using them only as libraries.
scenarios.role: OpenHands-specific maintainer guidance for backend, frontend, enterprise, skills/microagents, validation, and repository automation work.
scenarios.read_when: Use for OpenHands, agent canvas, app-server, sandbox, settings, secrets, conversation APIs, React frontend settings/chat UI, enterprise SaaS extension, OpenHands skills or microagents, pre-commit hooks, lockfiles, GitHub Actions pinning, issue triage automation, or repo validation commands.
scenarios.best_for: Changing the OpenHands checkout across Python backend routes/services, frontend TanStack Query/settings/i18n flows, enterprise auth/storage/integrations, prompt-extension files, or repo-wide maintenance automation.
scenarios.avoid_when: The task is only to operate OpenHands as an end user without editing the repository, or when another skill targets the exact library/framework being modified outside this checkout.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose openhands when the user is contributing to or troubleshooting the OpenHands repository itself, especially tasks involving app-server APIs, frontend settings or chat UI, enterprise SaaS code, OpenHands skills/microagents, validation commands, lockfiles, or issue automation.
